fields.Text:
Pediatric renal US with parenchymal indentation into renal sinus matching renal cortex. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Hypertrophied column of Bertin}}
fields.Extra: Q2. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Hypertrophied column of Bertin appears as a well-defined indentation in the renal sinus with echogenicity similar to adjacent renal parenchyma. It may contain hyperechoic areas representing interlobular vessels. It shows similar vascularity to normal renal parenchyma and can sometimes mimic a renal mass.

fields.Text:
Painless scrotal swelling with branching cystic mediastinum testis lesions. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Tubular ectasia of the rete testis}}
fields.Extra: Q3. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Tubular ectasia of rete testis is a benign condition caused by obstruction of efferent ducts. It appears as hypoechoic branching cystic lesions in the mediastinum testis with no blood flow on color Doppler.

fields.Text:
Chronic maxillary sinus pain worse with cold foods and CT dental disease. Source?

{{c1::Odontogenic maxillary sinusitis}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q4. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: In odontogenic maxillary sinusitis with identified dental pathology on imaging, treatment of the dental source of infection should be prioritized—dental surgery is the core component of management and failure to address the dental cause is associated with treatment failure.

fields.Text:
Postmenopausal bleeding with thickened/cystic endometrium in breast cancer therapy context. Key medication?

{{c1::Tamoxifen}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q6. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Tamoxifen increases endometrial cancer risk 2 to 3 fold, but routine ultrasound surveillance is not recommended for asymptomatic patients due to poor correlation between imaging findings and actual pathology.

fields.Text:
Incidental small benign-appearing bone lesion shown. Usual clinical significance?

{{c1::Frequently incidental}}
fields.Extra: Q7. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The sagittal uterine ultrasound shows a well circumscribed and hypoechoic intramural mass (blue arrow).

fields.Text:
Child with ankle pain and aggressive-appearing metaphyseal/diaphyseal bone lesion. Main mimic pair?

{{c1::Subacute osteomyelitis and eosinophilic granuloma}}

1/3
fields.Extra:
Q8. Correct; difficulty: hard. The radiograph shows a round, well-circumscribed lytic lesion in the distal fibular metaphysis (red arrows).

1/3

fields.Text:
Esophageal impression from posteriorly coursing vessel on swallow/CT. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Aberrant right subclavian artery}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q9. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Left aortic arch with aberrant right subclavian is the most common congenital arch anomaly. This entity is traditionally considered benign without vascular ring formation but can cause airway compression where the aberrant vessel crosses posterior to the trachea in more severe cases.

1/2

fields.Text:
Thyroid malignancy subtype most associated with microcalcifications?

{{c1::Papillary thyroid carcinoma}}
fields.Extra: Q13. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Papillary thyroid carcinoma is most associated with microcalcifications.

fields.Text:
Foot radiograph with punched-out erosions/overhanging edges. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Gout}}
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Classic radiographic findings of gout include punched-out lytic bone lesion, marginal erosions with overhanging edges, and soft tissue tophi.

fields.Text:
Atypical femoral fracture pattern in older woman. Likely underlying cause?

{{c1::Medication related}}
fields.Extra: Q15. Correct; difficulty: easy. Explanation: The frontal hip radiograph shows a focal lateral subtrochanteric periosteal reaction involving the femoral shaft (yellow arrow).

fields.Text:
Pelvic venous congestion with refluxing ovarian vein. Treatment?

{{c1::Transcatheter ovarian vein embolization}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q16. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Pelvic congestion syndrome is a condition that results from retrograde flow through incompetent valves in ovarian veins. It is a commonly missed and potentially treatable cause of chronic abdominopelvic pain characterized on angiogram by retrograde reflux, veins dilated to > 1 cm in diameter, and cross-pelvic collateral filling. Treatment is transcatheter ovarian vein embolization.

fields.Text:
Radionuclide decay scheme to Hg-201 with characteristic x-rays. Radionuclide?

{{c1::Tl-201}}
fields.Extra: Q18. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Tl-201 undergoes electron capture decay (Z-1) to Hg-201, producing mercury characteristic X-rays at 68-82 keV used for imaging.

fields.Text:
Incidental symmetric thyroid uptake on Tc-99m pertechnetate scan. Interpretation?

{{c1::Nonspecific thyroid uptake pattern}}
fields.Extra: Q19. Correct; difficulty: hard. The 99mTcO4 scan demonstrates markedly diminished uptake in the thyroid bed with focal uptake in the right parotid gland compatible with Warthin's tumor on the background of physiologic uptake in the salivary glands.

fields.Text:
Child with throat pain and CT tonsillar/peritonsillar inflammatory process without drainable abscess. Initial management?

{{c1::Antibiotic therapy}}
fields.Extra: Q21. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The CT image shows a well-defined rounded hypodense fluid collection with peripheral enhancement in the left parapharyngeal space just medial to the carotid artery.

fields.Text:
Macrocephaly, facial asymmetry, seizures, and enlarged dysplastic cerebral hemisphere. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Hemimegalencephaly}}
fields.Extra: Q22. Correct; difficulty: easy. T2-weighted MRI demonstrates an enlarged left cerebral hemisphere with cortical thickening (blue arrows) and underdeveloped sulci throughout.

fields.Text:
Pelvic fracture with CT active extravasation and hypotension. Next study?

{{c1::Catheter angiogram}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q23. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Pelvic fractures resulting from falls from height can often present with associated active extravasation arising from the pelvic vessels. Aggressive resuscitation, orthopedic stabilization of pelvic fractures and pelvic packing are primary treatment options that would usually precede endovascular treatment in hemodynamically unstable patients. In hemodynamically stable patients, catheter angiogram and possible embolization is performed.

fields.Text:
Second-trimester US with absent cranial vault. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Anencephaly}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q26. Correct; difficulty: easy. In anencephaly, there is complete absence of the neural tissue above the skull base and absence of the cranial vault.

fields.Text:
Suspicious solid breast US mass in a 36-year-old. Next step?

{{c1::Diagnostic mammogram and biopsy}}
fields.Extra: Q27. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: On ultrasound, a hypoechoic mass with a mostly irregular shape with orientation not parallel to skin, an indistinct border sometimes seen with halo, posterior shadowing, and small calcifications is highly suspicious for breast malignancy and should be followed by a biopsy.

fields.Text:
Follicular lymphoma follow-up with diffuse hepatic uptake/appearance after treatment. Etiology?

{{c1::Prior chemotherapy}}
fields.Extra: Q28. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The CT image demonstrates diffuse hepatic steatosis characterized by homogeneously decreased hepatic parenchymal attenuation (purple arrows).

fields.Text:
Cirrhosis with hepatic mass on US. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Hepatocellular carcinoma}}
fields.Extra: Q29. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The image demonstrates a large heterogeneous mass (red arrow) in the left lobe of the liver with a hypoechoic halo (blue arrows).

fields.Text:
Fever, leukocytosis, RUQ pain, and clustered rim-enhancing liver lesions. Organism?

{{c1::Escherichia coli}}
fields.Extra: Q31. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Coalescing pyogenic hepatic abscesses appear as multiple small hypoattenuating lesions with rim enhancement that merge into larger lesions, most commonly caused by Escherichia coli and Klebsiella pneumoniae from biliary sources.

fields.Text:
Bone scan/radiographs with hard chest wall mass and expansile rib lesion. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Fibrous dysplasia}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q32. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. The anterior and posterior oblique planar images from this delayed Tc99-MDP bone scan demonstrate intense tracer activity involving a single enlarged rib (yellow arrows).

1/2

fields.Text:
Fever, leukocytosis, RLQ pain, and complex adnexal inflammatory mass. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Tubo-ovarian abscess}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q34. Correct; difficulty: easy. The multiplanar CT images show a complex adnexal mass (blue arrows) with thick enhancing walls and septations (red arrows) adjacent to the right ovary (yellow arrow).

1/2

fields.Text:
Bilateral facial nerve weakness with enhancing cranial nerves and chronic lung disease. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Sarcoidosis}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q35. Correct; difficulty: moderate. There are two primary patterns of meningeal enhancement: pachymeningeal enhancement of the dura and leptomeningeal enhancement of the pia/subarachnoid.

fields.Text:
Inverse square law diagram: doubled distance from source. Dose change?

{{c1::One-fourth the dose}}
fields.Extra: Q36. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: The Inverse Square Law states that the intensity of the x-ray beam is inversely proportional to the square of the distance of the object from the source. There is a rapid decrease in beam intensity as the beam spreads out over an increasingly larger area.

fields.Text:
Recurrent UTIs, flank-to-groin pain, staghorn calculus. Urine pH?

{{c1::Alkaline urine}}
fields.Extra: Q37. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Staghorn renal calculi are most commonly composed of magnesium ammonium phosphate (struvite). They occur with increased ammonia production and alkaline urine. This is often due to infection with a urease-producing organism such as

fields.Text:
Diffuse pericardial thickening/calcification on CT in high-income country. Most common cause?

{{c1::Idiopathic/post-inflammatory}}
fields.Extra: Q39. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Idiopathic and post-inflammatory causes are most common in high-income countries, followed by prior cardiac surgery and radiation. Diffuse pericardial thickening with calcifications suggests constrictive pericarditis, but this remains a hemodynamic diagnosis. Echocardiographic confirmation of ventricular interdependence and respiratory variation is essential.

fields.Text:
Pediatric posterior fossa mass with dissemination workup images. Key prognostic factor?

{{c1::Surgical resectability}}

1/7
fields.Extra: Q40. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The images in the question stem depict left hepatic lobe biliary ductal dilatation and overlying capsular retraction secondary to the presence of an obstructing infiltrating mass located downstream from the obstructed bile ducts.

fields.Text:
Renal mass plus pulmonary cysts and perifollicular fibromas. Syndrome?

{{c1::Birt-Hogg-Dube syndrome}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q41. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Birt-Hogg-Dubé syndrome can present with perifollicular fibromas, pulmonary cysts, and renal masses (commonly chromophobe RCC and hybrid chromophobe-oncocytoma).

fields.Text:
Gymnast with progressive low back pain and pars defect. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Fracture}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q42. Correct; difficulty: easy. The coronal and axial SPECT from this Tc99m-MDP bone scan demonstrates bilateral tracer uptake at the pars interarticularis of the L5 vertebral body (blue arrows).

1/2

fields.Text:
Renal lesion with macroscopic fat, HU -60. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Angiomyolipoma}}
fields.Extra: Q43. Correct; difficulty: easy. The imaging finding of a renal mass with macroscopic fat (yellow arrows) is overwhelmingly likely to be an angiomyolipoma (AML).

fields.Text:
Child with vermian/posterior fossa tumor and restricted diffusion. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Medulloblastoma}}
fields.Extra: Q44. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Medulloblastoma shows restricted diffusion with reduced ADC values compared to other posterior fossa tumors due to densely packed small round cells. Typically arises from cerebellar vermis/roof of the fourth ventricle with variable enhancement patterns. Leptomeningeal seeding is common.

fields.Text:
Large rodlike breast calcifications. Associated entity?

{{c1::Ductal ectasia}}
fields.Extra: Q45. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Large rodlike calcifications are associated with ductal ectasia and inspissated secretions, typically benign in women >60 years with fatty breasts, occurring bilateral in 80% of cases.

fields.Text:
Painless afebrile scrotal swelling with vascular extratesticular mass. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Paratesticular rhabdomyosarcoma}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q46. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Paratesticular rhabdomyosarcoma appears as a large, ill-defined, heterogeneous, highly vascular mass in an extra-testicular location on ultrasound. It presents with a short history of painless unilateral scrotal lump and is highly aggressive, often with frequent regional lymph node metastases at diagnosis.

1/2

fields.Text:
Post-catheterization groin pseudoaneurysm. Treatment?

{{c1::Ultrasound-guided thrombin injection}}
fields.Extra: Q48. Correct; difficulty: easy. A contrast-filled vascular outpouching seen on the CT scan in the right medial thigh (red arrow) and to-and-fro flow on color Doppler ultrasound (the yin-yang sign, yellow arrow) are suggestive of the presence of a pseudoaneurysm.

fields.Text:
Bilateral pleural/diaphragmatic calcified plaques. Exposure?

{{c1::Asbestos}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q49. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Asbestos-related pleural calcifications appear as bilateral flat calcified plaques along the posterolateral parietal pleura and diaphragms with characteristic holly leaf irregular edges on radiographs.They spare the costophrenic angles and lung apices, distinguishing them from other causes of pleural calcification.

fields.Text:
Synovial hemosiderin process. Expected MRI signal?

{{c1::Low T1, low T2, variable enhancement}}
fields.Extra: Q50. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Classic uterine fibroids appear as smoothly circumscribed, homogeneous masses that are T1 isointense to mildly hypointense and characteristically T2 hypointense compared to myometrium, with early homogeneous enhancement and low signal on diffusion-weighted imaging.

fields.Text: Suspected Boerhaave syndrome after forceful vomiting is evaluated first with {{c1::water-soluble contrast esophagram}}.
fields.Extra: Q1. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Boerhaave syndrome carries high mortality when diagnosis is delayed. Water-soluble contrast esophagram — fluoroscopic or CT-based — is the study of choice. Barium is avoided acutely due to mediastinitis risk from extravasation, but may be used after negative initial water-soluble esophagram.

fields.Text: A hypertrophied column of Bertin has echogenicity matching adjacent renal cortex and represents {{c1::normal cortical tissue indenting the renal sinus}}.
fields.Extra:
Q2. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Hypertrophied column of Bertin appears as a well-defined indentation in the renal sinus with echogenicity similar to adjacent renal parenchyma. It may contain hyperechoic areas representing interlobular vessels. It shows similar vascularity to normal renal parenchyma and can sometimes mimic a renal mass.

1/2

fields.Text: Tubular ectasia of the rete testis is a benign condition with {{c1::branching cystic lesions in the mediastinum testis}} and no Doppler flow.
fields.Extra: Q3. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Tubular ectasia of rete testis is a benign condition caused by obstruction of efferent ducts. It appears as hypoechoic branching cystic lesions in the mediastinum testis with no blood flow on color Doppler.

fields.Text: Odontogenic maxillary sinusitis requires treatment of the {{c1::dental source}} rather than sinus therapy alone.
fields.Extra:
Q4. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: In odontogenic maxillary sinusitis with identified dental pathology on imaging, treatment of the dental source of infection should be prioritized—dental surgery is the core component of management and failure to address the dental cause is associated with treatment failure.

1/2

fields.Text: Requiring remediation after a failed rotation exam reflects commitment to {{c1::professional competence}}.
fields.Extra: Q5. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The attending radiologist is regulating the profession by ensuring that their resident meets predetermined educational standards.

fields.Text: Tamoxifen increases endometrial cancer risk and can cause {{c1::thickened/cystic endometrium}} in postmenopausal patients.
fields.Extra:
Q6. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Tamoxifen increases endometrial cancer risk 2 to 3 fold, but routine ultrasound surveillance is not recommended for asymptomatic patients due to poor correlation between imaging findings and actual pathology.

1/2

fields.Text: Incidental benign bone lesions are often managed with {{c1::recognition/reassurance}} when imaging features are classic.
fields.Extra:
Q7. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The sagittal uterine ultrasound shows a well circumscribed and hypoechoic intramural mass (blue arrow).

1/2

fields.Text: In children, {{c1::subacute osteomyelitis and eosinophilic granuloma}} can be difficult to distinguish clinically and radiographically.
fields.Extra:
Q8. Correct; difficulty: hard. The radiograph shows a round, well-circumscribed lytic lesion in the distal fibular metaphysis (red arrows).

1/6

fields.Text: A left aortic arch with aberrant right subclavian artery may cause dysphagia/globus by passing {{c1::posterior to the esophagus}}.
fields.Extra:
Q9. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Left aortic arch with aberrant right subclavian is the most common congenital arch anomaly. This entity is traditionally considered benign without vascular ring formation but can cause airway compression where the aberrant vessel crosses posterior to the trachea in more severe cases.

1/4

fields.Text: Admission to a medical staff or health-care-provider panel is {{c1::credentialing}}.
fields.Extra: Q10. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. There are two distinct but generally linked regulatory processes involved in obtaining the ability to practice medicine.

fields.Text: For low-bleeding-risk IR procedures in chronic liver disease, SIR considers INR {{c1::nonapplicable}} and platelets >20,000/uL acceptable.
fields.Extra: Q11. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: The Society of Interventional Radiology considers INR nonapplicable, and platelet count of more than 20,000 /uL acceptable for patients with chronic liver disease undergoing a procedure with low bleeding risk.

fields.Text: A procedural checklist is a human-factors example of {{c1::standardization}}.
fields.Extra: Q12. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Human factor engineering is a discipline in which challenges involving health care providers and safely using available medical equipment and technology are explored.

fields.Text: Thyroid microcalcifications are most associated with {{c1::papillary thyroid carcinoma}}.
fields.Extra: Q13. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Papillary thyroid carcinoma is most associated with microcalcifications.

fields.Text: Gout classically causes {{c1::punched-out marginal erosions with overhanging edges}} and soft tissue tophi.
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Classic radiographic findings of gout include punched-out lytic bone lesion, marginal erosions with overhanging edges, and soft tissue tophi.

fields.Text: Atypical femoral fractures are classically associated with {{c1::bisphosphonate or other antiresorptive therapy}}.
fields.Extra:
Q15. Correct; difficulty: easy. Explanation: The frontal hip radiograph shows a focal lateral subtrochanteric periosteal reaction involving the femoral shaft (yellow arrow).

1/2

fields.Text: Pelvic congestion syndrome is treated with {{c1::transcatheter ovarian vein embolization}}.
fields.Extra:
Q16. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Pelvic congestion syndrome is a condition that results from retrograde flow through incompetent valves in ovarian veins. It is a commonly missed and potentially treatable cause of chronic abdominopelvic pain characterized on angiogram by retrograde reflux, veins dilated to > 1 cm in diameter, and cross-pelvic collateral filling. Treatment is transcatheter ovarian vein embolization.

1/2

fields.Text: Average annual U.S. radiation exposure is approximately {{c1::5-6 mSv}}.
fields.Extra: Q17. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: The average annual U.S. radiation dose is between 5 and 6 mSv, according to the 2025 ABR RISC Study Guide. Of the 5-6 mSv, 3.1 mSv comes from natural background radiation, and the remainder comes from medical, commercial, and industrial activities.

fields.Text: Tl-201 decays by {{c1::electron capture}} to Hg-201 and is imaged via mercury characteristic x-rays.
fields.Extra: Q18. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Tl-201 undergoes electron capture decay (Z-1) to Hg-201, producing mercury characteristic X-rays at 68-82 keV used for imaging.

fields.Text: Tc-99m pertechnetate thyroid uptake patterns can be nonspecific; incidental thyroid activity can reflect {{c1::multiple benign or malignant thyroid conditions}}.
fields.Extra: Q19. Correct; difficulty: hard. The 99mTcO4 scan demonstrates markedly diminished uptake in the thyroid bed with focal uptake in the right parotid gland compatible with Warthin's tumor on the background of physiologic uptake in the salivary glands.

fields.Text: The cardiac MRI short-axis plane is perpendicular to the {{c1::long axis of the heart}} through the mid-LV.
fields.Extra: Q20. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Standard imaging planes in cardiac MRI include the short axis, horizontal long axis (four-chamber view), and vertical long axis (two-chamber view). These planes are obtained by drawing a line from the mitral valve to the left ventricular apex; this defines the long axis of the heart. The short axis plane passes perpendicular to the long axis of the heart and through the level of the mid-left ventricle.

fields.Text: Peritonsillar cellulitis/phlegmon without a drainable abscess is initially managed with {{c1::antibiotic therapy}}.
fields.Extra: Q21. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The CT image shows a well-defined rounded hypodense fluid collection with peripheral enhancement in the left parapharyngeal space just medial to the carotid artery.

fields.Text: Hemimegalencephaly presents with macrocephaly, seizures, and {{c1::enlargement/dysplasia of one cerebral hemisphere}}.
fields.Extra:
Q22. Correct; difficulty: easy. T2-weighted MRI demonstrates an enlarged left cerebral hemisphere with cortical thickening (blue arrows) and underdeveloped sulci throughout.

1/2

fields.Text: Pelvic fracture with hemodynamic instability and active extravasation should proceed to {{c1::catheter angiography/embolization}}.
fields.Extra:
Q23. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Pelvic fractures resulting from falls from height can often present with associated active extravasation arising from the pelvic vessels. Aggressive resuscitation, orthopedic stabilization of pelvic fractures and pelvic packing are primary treatment options that would usually precede endovascular treatment in hemodynamically unstable patients. In hemodynamically stable patients, catheter angiogram and possible embolization is performed.

1/2

fields.Text: Physician self-referral is discouraged by {{c1::ACR ethics}}.
fields.Extra: Q24. Correct; difficulty: easy. Medical professionals and their organizations have many opportunities to compromise their professional responsibilities by pursuing private gain or personal advantage.

fields.Text: The Image Gently Step Lightly campaign focuses on reducing radiation dose during {{c1::fluoroscopically guided pediatric IR procedures}}.
fields.Extra: Q25. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The campaign “Step Lightly” was published in 2009 by the Journal of Vascular and Interventional Radiology and furthered the principles of “Image Gently.” A pre-procedural checklist was created for review by the pediatric interventional radiology team.

fields.Text: Anencephaly is characterized by absence of the {{c1::cranial vault and cerebral hemispheres}}.
fields.Extra:
Q26. Correct; difficulty: easy. In anencephaly, there is complete absence of the neural tissue above the skull base and absence of the cranial vault.

1/3

fields.Text: A suspicious breast US mass in a woman age 30 or older should get {{c1::diagnostic mammography and biopsy}}.
fields.Extra: Q27. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: On ultrasound, a hypoechoic mass with a mostly irregular shape with orientation not parallel to skin, an indistinct border sometimes seen with halo, posterior shadowing, and small calcifications is highly suspicious for breast malignancy and should be followed by a biopsy.

fields.Text: Diffuse hepatic FDG uptake or altered liver appearance shortly after lymphoma treatment may be due to {{c1::recent chemotherapy}}.
fields.Extra:
Q28. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The CT image demonstrates diffuse hepatic steatosis characterized by homogeneously decreased hepatic parenchymal attenuation (purple arrows).

1/2

fields.Text: In cirrhosis, a new suspicious hepatic lesion is {{c1::hepatocellular carcinoma}} until proven otherwise.
fields.Extra:
Q29. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The image demonstrates a large heterogeneous mass (red arrow) in the left lobe of the liver with a hypoechoic halo (blue arrows).

1/2

fields.Text: The fetal four-chamber view best evaluates {{c1::atrioventricular septal defects}}.
fields.Extra:
Q30. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: In addition to four-chamber views, outflow tract views allow for more thorough diagnosis of Tetralogy of Fallot, transposition of the great arteries and truncus arteriosus at fetal ultrasound.

1/2

fields.Text: Pyogenic hepatic abscesses are commonly caused by {{c1::E. coli and Klebsiella}} from biliary/portal sources.
fields.Extra:
Q31. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Coalescing pyogenic hepatic abscesses appear as multiple small hypoattenuating lesions with rim enhancement that merge into larger lesions, most commonly caused by Escherichia coli and Klebsiella pneumoniae from biliary sources.

1/2

fields.Text: Fibrous dysplasia can present as an expansile rib/chest wall lesion with {{c1::increased uptake on bone scan}}.
fields.Extra:
Q32. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. The anterior and posterior oblique planar images from this delayed Tc99-MDP bone scan demonstrate intense tracer activity involving a single enlarged rib (yellow arrows).

1/4

fields.Text: Instrument and radiation survey calibration records must be kept for {{c1::3 years}}.
fields.Extra: Q33. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. According to the 2025 ABR RISC study guide, records of instrument and radiation survey calibrations must be kept for 3 years.

fields.Text: A complex adnexal inflammatory mass with fever/leukocytosis suggests {{c1::tubo-ovarian abscess}}.
fields.Extra:
Q34. Correct; difficulty: easy. The multiplanar CT images show a complex adnexal mass (blue arrows) with thick enhancing walls and septations (red arrows) adjacent to the right ovary (yellow arrow).

1/4

fields.Text: Neurosarcoidosis can cause {{c1::bilateral facial nerve enhancement/palsy}}.
fields.Extra:
Q35. Correct; difficulty: moderate. There are two primary patterns of meningeal enhancement: pachymeningeal enhancement of the dura and leptomeningeal enhancement of the pia/subarachnoid.

1/2

fields.Text: By the inverse square law, doubling distance from the source reduces exposure to {{c1::one-fourth}}.
fields.Extra: Q36. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: The Inverse Square Law states that the intensity of the x-ray beam is inversely proportional to the square of the distance of the object from the source. There is a rapid decrease in beam intensity as the beam spreads out over an increasingly larger area.

fields.Text: Struvite/staghorn calculi occur with urease-producing infection and {{c1::alkaline urine}}.
fields.Extra: Q37. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Staghorn renal calculi are most commonly composed of magnesium ammonium phosphate (struvite). They occur with increased ammonia production and alkaline urine. This is often due to infection with a urease-producing organism such as

fields.Text: Low-contrast CT visibility is increased by {{c1::narrowing the window width}}.
fields.Extra: Q38. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Low-contrast visibility improves with narrower window width, higher tube current (mAs), contrast agents, thicker slices, softer filters, and lower kV - each trading off spatial resolution, dose, or dynamic range.

fields.Text: In high-income countries, constrictive pericarditis is most commonly {{c1::idiopathic or post-inflammatory}}.
fields.Extra: Q39. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Idiopathic and post-inflammatory causes are most common in high-income countries, followed by prior cardiac surgery and radiation. Diffuse pericardial thickening with calcifications suggests constrictive pericarditis, but this remains a hemodynamic diagnosis. Echocardiographic confirmation of ventricular interdependence and respiratory variation is essential.

fields.Text: The most important prognostic factor for many pediatric solid tumors is {{c1::surgical resectability}}.
fields.Extra:
Q40. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The images in the question stem depict left hepatic lobe biliary ductal dilatation and overlying capsular retraction secondary to the presence of an obstructing infiltrating mass located downstream from the obstructed bile ducts.

1/8

fields.Text: Birt-Hogg-Dube syndrome causes fibrofolliculomas, pulmonary cysts/pneumothorax, and {{c1::renal tumors}}.
fields.Extra:
Q41. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Birt-Hogg-Dubé syndrome can present with perifollicular fibromas, pulmonary cysts, and renal masses (commonly chromophobe RCC and hybrid chromophobe-oncocytoma).

1/3

fields.Text: Adolescent gymnast with progressive low back pain and pars defect has {{c1::spondylolysis/fracture}}.
fields.Extra:
Q42. Correct; difficulty: easy. The coronal and axial SPECT from this Tc99m-MDP bone scan demonstrates bilateral tracer uptake at the pars interarticularis of the L5 vertebral body (blue arrows).

1/4

fields.Text: A renal lesion with macroscopic fat, such as HU -60, is {{c1::angiomyolipoma}}.
fields.Extra:
Q43. Correct; difficulty: easy. The imaging finding of a renal mass with macroscopic fat (yellow arrows) is overwhelmingly likely to be an angiomyolipoma (AML).

1/2

fields.Text: Medulloblastoma often arises from the cerebellar vermis/fourth ventricle roof and shows {{c1::restricted diffusion}}.
fields.Extra:
Q44. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Medulloblastoma shows restricted diffusion with reduced ADC values compared to other posterior fossa tumors due to densely packed small round cells. Typically arises from cerebellar vermis/roof of the fourth ventricle with variable enhancement patterns. Leptomeningeal seeding is common.

1/2

fields.Text: Large rodlike breast calcifications are benign calcifications associated with {{c1::ductal ectasia}}.
fields.Extra: Q45. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Large rodlike calcifications are associated with ductal ectasia and inspissated secretions, typically benign in women >60 years with fatty breasts, occurring bilateral in 80% of cases.

fields.Text: Paratesticular rhabdomyosarcoma is a painless extratesticular mass in children with {{c1::marked vascularity}}.
fields.Extra:
Q46. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Paratesticular rhabdomyosarcoma appears as a large, ill-defined, heterogeneous, highly vascular mass in an extra-testicular location on ultrasound. It presents with a short history of painless unilateral scrotal lump and is highly aggressive, often with frequent regional lymph node metastases at diagnosis.

1/4

fields.Text: MRI is the best modality for evaluating local soft-tissue invasion by a {{c1::Pancoast/superior sulcus tumor}}.
fields.Extra: Q47. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Superior sulcus tumors (Pancoast tumors) invade the apical chest wall with potential to invade nearby structures. As a result, this can cause characteristic symptoms. MRI is the optimal modality for evaluating the soft tissue involvement of these tumors and determining their ability to be resected.

fields.Text: Post-catheterization femoral pseudoaneurysm is treated with {{c1::ultrasound-guided thrombin injection}} when suitable.
fields.Extra:
Q48. Correct; difficulty: easy. A contrast-filled vascular outpouching seen on the CT scan in the right medial thigh (red arrow) and to-and-fro flow on color Doppler ultrasound (the yin-yang sign, yellow arrow) are suggestive of the presence of a pseudoaneurysm.

1/2

fields.Text: Asbestos exposure causes bilateral calcified pleural plaques, often involving the {{c1::diaphragmatic pleura}}.
fields.Extra:
Q49. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Asbestos-related pleural calcifications appear as bilateral flat calcified plaques along the posterolateral parietal pleura and diaphragms with characteristic holly leaf irregular edges on radiographs.They spare the costophrenic angles and lung apices, distinguishing them from other causes of pleural calcification.

1/2

fields.Text: Hemosiderin-rich synovial disease shows {{c1::low T1, low T2 signal}} with variable enhancement.
fields.Extra:
Q50. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Classic uterine fibroids appear as smoothly circumscribed, homogeneous masses that are T1 isointense to mildly hypointense and characteristically T2 hypointense compared to myometrium, with early homogeneous enhancement and low signal on diffusion-weighted imaging.

1/2

fields.Text: Credentialing is admission to a provider panel or medical staff; {{c1::privileging}} is authorization to perform specific procedures.
fields.Extra: Q10. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. There are two distinct but generally linked regulatory processes involved in obtaining the ability to practice medicine.

fields.Text: Peritonsillar inflammation without a mature/drainable abscess is treated first with {{c1::antibiotics}}, not drainage.
fields.Extra: Q21. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The CT image shows a well-defined rounded hypodense fluid collection with peripheral enhancement in the left parapharyngeal space just medial to the carotid artery.

fields.Text: Step Lightly is the pediatric IR radiation-safety campaign for {{c1::reducing fluoroscopy dose}}.
fields.Extra: Q25. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The campaign “Step Lightly” was published in 2009 by the Journal of Vascular and Interventional Radiology and furthered the principles of “Image Gently.” A pre-procedural checklist was created for review by the pediatric interventional radiology team.

fields.Text: Recent chemotherapy can explain diffuse hepatic imaging changes in lymphoma follow-up; this is not primarily {{c1::hepatic lymphoma progression}}.
fields.Extra:
Q28. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The CT image demonstrates diffuse hepatic steatosis characterized by homogeneously decreased hepatic parenchymal attenuation (purple arrows).

1/2

fields.Text: The most common organisms in pyogenic liver abscess are {{c1::E. coli and Klebsiella}}, not Entamoeba in typical U.S. biliary-source cases.
fields.Extra:
Q31. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Coalescing pyogenic hepatic abscesses appear as multiple small hypoattenuating lesions with rim enhancement that merge into larger lesions, most commonly caused by Escherichia coli and Klebsiella pneumoniae from biliary sources.

1/2

fields.Text: An expansile rib lesion with bone-scan uptake and ground-glass/fibro-osseous appearance favors {{c1::fibrous dysplasia}}.
fields.Extra:
Q32. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. The anterior and posterior oblique planar images from this delayed Tc99-MDP bone scan demonstrate intense tracer activity involving a single enlarged rib (yellow arrows).

1/4

fields.Text: Instrument and radiation survey calibration records are retained for {{c1::3 years}}.
fields.Extra: Q33. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. According to the 2025 ABR RISC study guide, records of instrument and radiation survey calibrations must be kept for 3 years.

fields.Text: Constrictive pericarditis in high-income countries is most commonly {{c1::idiopathic/post-inflammatory}}, not tuberculosis.
fields.Extra: Q39. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Idiopathic and post-inflammatory causes are most common in high-income countries, followed by prior cardiac surgery and radiation. Diffuse pericardial thickening with calcifications suggests constrictive pericarditis, but this remains a hemodynamic diagnosis. Echocardiographic confirmation of ventricular interdependence and respiratory variation is essential.

fields.Text: For the lesion shown, prognosis depends most on {{c1::surgical resectability}} rather than age alone.
fields.Extra:
Q40. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. The images in the question stem depict left hepatic lobe biliary ductal dilatation and overlying capsular retraction secondary to the presence of an obstructing infiltrating mass located downstream from the obstructed bile ducts.

1/8
